findings.summary: review-gate red: implementation path changed with no review record; resolved by this record

D4 said EVERY L4 arm must load both extensions, contradicting the AGENTS.md-only contrast arm and the pre-upgrade half; would have made the can-fail arm unable to fail (ADR-0050). Scoped to vfkb-present arms
RFC declared status Ratified, not the documented RFC->ADR lifecycle (docs/rfc/README.md:21, ADR-0007); binding constraint lived only in a mutable file. Wrote ADR-0066, indexed in both READMEs
PR head had no CI runs; displayed green checks belonged to an older sha. Now verified per-sha via gh api check-runs
pi-extension.ts stated the gating guarantee unscoped; gating.ts:7-13 limits it to write/edit tools (exec out of scope, issue #151)
comment attributed the captured payload to one event; tool_input from tool_execution_start is a second route. Labelled UNVERIFIED which route carried it in the #150 repro
RFC-036 and RFC-037 missing from docs/rfc/README.md index

both docs mis-cited pi.Dockerfile as injecting DEEPSEEK_TOKEN; actual site is l4-purpose.mjs:278, whose '|| empty-string' fallback hides a missing secret where the Claude path throws (ADR-0051 clause 3 quiet-success). Corrected and the defect recorded for milestone 1
RFC frontmatter status lacked the ADR pointer used by RFC-033/034/035
D5 nested under Non-goals, rendering the repo-name ruling outside Decisions in any TOC
uncited pi release-pace claim; CHANGELOG.md:3,940 gives twelve minor bumps in ~7 weeks
CI-automatable claim addressed only the credential half; RFC-036:75 records ARC capacity absent and kagent unratified for this use
ADR stated a harness-scoped bridge result unscoped; -e path is the ADR-0051 clause 1 trap this ADR names
D3 blockquote lacked the scoping qualifier D4 supplies
source-type count and RFC-036 section attribution both loose

ADR code span used backslash-escaped backticks, invalid in CommonMark; rendered broken in a body ADR-0001 makes immutable. Fixed and re-rendered through GitHub /markdown API to confirm a single clean code span
packages.md:164 covers npm and git only, not all source types
D1 predated the git-only ruling; annotated rather than rewritten
RFC retained the unscoped 'works in pi today' the ADR forbids
latent-defect entry cited line numbers that hold the corrected comment post-merge

ruledOut: no capability declared done/shipped: ADR-0066 states the pi package is not delivered until its install-path L4 record exists (ADR-0050, ADR-0051 clause 2)
ADR-0001 intact: only the new ADR-0066 added, no existing ADR body edited
no AI attribution in any branch commit; no secrets
no behavioural code change: comment-stripped src is byte-identical to the parent
ADR and RFC agree on all five decisions incl. the both-extensions scoping
every file:line citation in both documents verified against ground truth
